vulns.desc: Server-Side Request Forgery: GET /fetch?url= (and /preview?url=) fetches an arbitrary URL server-side. The blocklist only blocks literal 127.0.0.1/localhost, so cloud-internal hosts (169.254.169.254, instance-data) and alternate encodings slip through. This is the ENTRY of the chain.
vulns.exploit: GET /fetch?url=http://example.com -> server-side request issued. GET /fetch?url=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ -> reaches cloud metadata (see K2).

vulns.desc: Cloud instance-role credential disclosure via the SSRF (chain step 2): pivoting the SSRF to the IMDS endpoint http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/<role> returns live AccessKeyId / SecretAccessKey / session Token. Only reachable by exploiting K1 — IMDS is not directly reachable by the attacker.

OS command injection -> RCE (chain step 3): /internal/admin?cmd= executes a maintenance command but is gated by a valid instance credential — it trusts the IMDS session Token leaked in K2 as a Bearer. Only reachable after chaining K1->K2 to obtain the Token. (Lab-safe: only a read-only canary actually runs.)
vulns.exploit: GET /internal/admin?cmd=whoami with Authorization: Bearer <Token-from-K2> -> command output (RCE). Non-canary commands are confirmed injectable but intercepted.

vulns.desc: Security misconfiguration (app-wide): debug mode on -> malformed input returns a full Python traceback in the response body, leaking source paths and internals.
vulns.exploit: GET /fetch?url=http://a:notaport/ -> ValueError stack trace (full traceback) in the response.

vulns.desc: Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any endpoint -> none of CSP/XFO/XCTO/HSTS present.
